mappings.rationaleAI: Core text: covers all major planets, their surfaces and atmospheres (C.1, C.3), minor planets and the Kuiper belt (A.3.a), regularities of the solar system (A.4), and theories of solar system origin (A.6).

Core text for subsection B: covers the Sun's photosphere, chromosphere, and corona (B.1), magnetic fields and sunspots (B.2), solar interior and energy generation (B.3), and solar-terrestrial relationships (B.5).
Core text for subsection E: covers the Moon's physical nature and surface features (E.4), planetary satellites (C), tidal heating (E.7), and Pluto and Kuiper belt objects (C.3.e).
Covers the solar system as a planetary system (A), theories of origin (A.6), regularities and interactions among solar system bodies (A.4-A.5), and telescopic observation methods.
Covers the Earth as a planet (D), its physical properties and internal structure (D.4-D.5), radioactive decay relevant to dating (E.5.b), and the solar wind's effect on Earth (B.5).
Covers planetary atmospheres (C.1), the Moon (E), plate tectonics and surface features of terrestrial planets (C.1), and conditions on Mars and other solar system bodies.
Relevant to planetary atmospheres (C.1, C.3), the Earth's atmosphere (D.6), the electromagnetic spectrum in solar radiation (B.4), and solar-terrestrial interactions (B.5).
Covers gravitational interactions among solar system bodies (A.5), orbital mechanics and perturbations, and the gravitational field of the Moon (E.3).
Relevant to the Sun as a star, solar energy generation and nuclear fusion (B.3), and stellar evolution providing context for the Sun's evolution (B.3).
Relevant to the Earth's magnetism (D.4), the Sun's magnetic fields and centres of activity (B.2.a), and solar-terrestrial magnetic interactions (B.5).
Relevant to the chemical analysis of lunar rock samples (E.6.a) and the mineral composition of meteorites (A.3.c), including crystal structure and X-ray analysis methods.
Relevant to the composition of lunar rocks (E.6), meteorites (A.3.c), and the igneous, metamorphic, and sedimentary processes on terrestrial planets (C.1).
Relevant to radiometric dating of meteorites and lunar samples for determining solar system age (A.3.c, E.6), and nuclear energy processes in the Sun (B.3).
Relevant to solar radiation (B.4), aberration of light (D.3.b), and the observational optics used to study the Sun, planets, and Moon.
Covers the optical and radio telescopes used for planetary observation, solar study, and the remote observation of solar system bodies (A, B, C).
Covers the physics of the Sun's interior and energy generation (B.3), the electromagnetic spectrum of solar radiation (B.4), and angular momentum in solar system formation (A.6).
Relevant to solar-terrestrial relationships (B.5), the solar wind's effects on Earth's atmosphere, and the atmospheric conditions on terrestrial planets (C.1).
Relevant to solar-terrestrial relationships (B.5), the effects of Earth's orbital position on climate (D.2-D.3), and atmospheric composition of terrestrial planets.
Relevant to the Sun-Earth-Moon system as the astronomical basis of chronology (E.7.a.ii), celestial motions, and the historical understanding of the solar system's geometry.
Relevant to surface features of the Earth as a planet (D.6), radiometric dating methods, and geomorphological processes on terrestrial planet surfaces.
Relevant to the Earth as a planet (D), the origin of Earth's atmosphere and hydrosphere (D.6), and solar energy's role in Earth system processes (B.5).
Relevant to isotopic analysis of lunar samples and meteorites (E.6, A.3.c) used to determine ages and origins of solar system materials.
